title: Optimizing a React Memoized Component Re-renders on State Change Due to Dependent Arrays

statement.markdown: You are given a React function component `Counter` that displays two counters. The component uses React's `useMemo` hook to memoize the counters' values based on the state `counters`. However, when the state `counters` changes, the component re-renders unnecessarily. Your task is to fix the bug by optimizing the memoization of the counters' values.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an object with one property: `counters` - an array of objects with two properties: `id` and `value`.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be an array of two numbers.
statement.notes: The component should only re-render when the counters' values change.

examples.explanation: When the state `counters` does not change, the component should not re-render.
When the state `counters` changes, the component should re-render with the updated values.

hints.text: Check the dependencies used in the `useMemo` hook.
Consider using a different approach to memoize the counters' values.

editorial.title: Optimizing Memoization in React Components
editorial.intuition: The `useMemo` hook is used to memoize the counters' values based on the state `counters`. However, when the state `counters` changes, the component re-renders unnecessarily.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible solution is to use the `useEffect` hook to update the counters' values only when the state `counters` changes.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use a different approach to memoize the counters' values, such as using the `useReducer` hook to update the counters' values only when the state `counters` changes.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the `useReducer` hook to update the counters' values only when the state `counters` changes, and to use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the counters' values based on the state `counters`.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correct solution can be proven by showing that the component only re-renders when the counters' values change.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the correct solution will show that the component only re-renders when the counters' values change.
commonMistakes: Not using the `useReducer` hook to update the counters' values.
Not using the `useMemo` hook to memoize the counters' values.
